summary: Matched a large block of game-mode result/stat accessor functions in src/melee/gm/gm_17AD.c, replacing address-only placeholders with C implementations and updating prototypes and MatchPlayerData layout. The automated decomp.dev report credited +3112 matched bytes: 30 new full matches and 4 remaining partial matches at 92.31%. The PR author noted unresolved issues around inlines and an slwi in a couple functions, so the evidence supports that the merged code was mostly matching but not a complete cleanup of every function in the range.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_17AD functions from fn_8017AD04 through fn_8017B9F4 in src/melee/gm/gm_17AD.c.
Added local includes needed by the implementation: gm/forward.h, placeholder.h, gm/gmresult.h, gm/types.h, and lb/lblanguage.h.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in gm_17AD.h with concrete s32-returning prototypes, usually taking a single s32 argument; fn_8017AD04 takes two s32 arguments.
Expanded struct MatchPlayerData in src/melee/gm/types.h to expose fields used by result-stat accessors, including x16, x18, x1C, x20, x40 through xA4, and adjusted padding.
Added dolphin/types.h include to gm/types.h for fixed-width integer types used by the new struct fields.
Implemented repeated stat clamping patterns with limits such as 9999, 99999, 999999, and 9999999.
Implemented match-end player standings access through fn_80174274() and me->player_standings[slot/index].
Implemented several per-player kill/self-destruct/stat accessors, including slot_type checks that return -1 for invalid or CPU-like slot conditions in some functions.
Implemented language-dependent unit conversions in fn_8017B410, fn_8017B91C, and fn_8017B9F4 using lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS(), 30.4788, and 100.0/100 divisors.
The decomp.dev bot reported 30 new full matches and 4 partial improvements for fn_8017B07C, fn_8017B0E4, fn_8017B14C, and fn_8017B1B4.
smart_moves: Promoted unknown header declarations to concrete s32 prototypes, reducing reliance on UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS for this gm_17AD range.
Expanded MatchPlayerData only as far as needed by the matched result-stat accessors, preserving offset-style names where semantics were not yet known.
Used address-based function names consistently for still-unnamed functions, avoiding speculative semantic naming.
Factored the symmetric bounded clamp used by multiple accessors into fn_8017AD04 and prevented unwanted inlining with #pragma dont_inline on/reset.
Used PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(24) in functions that needed stack-shape assistance for matching.
Matched subtle numeric conversion behavior with explicit casts through f32/u32/s32 in the language-dependent unit conversion functions.
decomp_lessons: For result/stat accessor clusters, matching one function often requires exposing a contiguous region of the backing struct; here, MatchPlayerData padding was split into typed offset fields so loads from x40-xA4 could be expressed directly.
Avoid inventing semantic names for fields like x1C, x40, x94, or x98 when the PR evidence only proves offsets and access patterns.
Small clamp helpers may be inlined by the compiler unless explicitly controlled; this PR used #pragma dont_inline for fn_8017AD04 and still documented remaining inline uncertainty.
When many functions share the same clamp-after-load pattern, consistent local variable naming and control-flow shape can help match repeated codegen.
Language or region conditionals can change arithmetic lowering; preserving exact float constants and cast order is important for matching conversion functions.
Partial matches around similar slot-indexed functions may indicate unresolved indexing/codegen details such as slwi emission, not necessarily incorrect high-level logic.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address names such as fn_8017AD04, fn_8017B410, and fn_8017B9F4.
Unidentified struct fields used offset-style names such as x1C, x20, x5C, x94, and xA4.
Padding fields retained offset-based pad names such as pad_x30.
Known project types and functions were used where available: MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, fn_80174274, lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS.
Header prototypes kept the project address-comment convention, e.g. /* 17AD04 */ s32 fn_8017AD04(s32, s32);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on/reset was applied around fn_8017AD04 to stop other functions from inlining the clamp helper.
PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(24) were inserted in functions where stack padding was needed for matching.
Explicit clamp branches were written instead of abstract helper calls for many direct accessors, preserving compare/return structure.
Float conversion functions used exact constants and cast ordering, including 30.4788f/30.4788 and conversions through f32 and u32.
Slot-specific functions used literal slot checks against 0, 1, 2, or 3 and slot_type != 3 conditions to match observed control flow.
The author explicitly noted unresolved inlines and an slwi in a couple functions; the bot report corroborates that four functions remained partial rather than full matches.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body says: "couldnt figure out the inlines and the slwi in a couple functions", documenting known remaining matching issues.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code increased to 35.63%, +0.08%, +3112 bytes.
Automated report listed 30 new full matches in main/melee/gm/gm_17AD.
Automated report listed 4 partial improvements at 92.31%: fn_8017B07C, fn_8017B0E4, fn_8017B14C, and fn_8017B1B4.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched fn_8017B07C, fn_8017B0E4, fn_8017B14C, or fn_8017B1B4.
Search for later renames or documentation of MatchPlayerData fields x1C, x20, x5C, x94, and x98.
Look for follow-up fixes involving gm_17AD slwi codegen or inlining around fn_8017AD04.
Compare later gm_17AD.c implementations to see whether #pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK usage was removed after better struct typing.
